bugs.title: BuiltInRegistries.ITEM.get return type not guarded by //? in RegistrationGametest equality check
bugs.evidence:
val item = BuiltInRegistries.ITEM.get(id)
val airItem = BuiltInRegistries.ITEM.get(airId)
if (item == airItem) { ... }
Return type varies: Item (1.20.1-1.21.1), Optional<Holder<Item>> (1.21.2-1.21.10), Item/Holder<Item> (1.21.11+). Direct equality comparison `item == airItem` is unsafe across epoch boundaries — e.g. on 1.21.2-1.21.10 both sides are Optional, so comparison may always be false (two distinct Optional instances wrapping equal content) unless .equals() is structurally delegated, or may succeed coincidentally. No //? fork guards this comparison.
bugs.expected: The equality check should be version-guarded. For 1.21.2-1.21.10 (Optional<Holder<Item>>) the comparison of two Optional instances via == will use referential equality in Kotlin unless Optional.equals() delegates to the wrapped value. On 1.20.1-1.21.1 (direct Item) and 1.21.11+ (Holder<Item> or direct), the comparison semantics differ. Plan note on line 549 acknowledges this and says 'verify the BuiltInRegistries.ITEM.get return type on each MC version — comparison may need .value() on some versions where the return is Holder<Item> or Optional<Holder<Item>>. If so, refine the equality check per-epoch.'

bugs.title: iconStack() in DndWeaponsMod uses .map { ItemStack(it) } where it is Holder<Item> — ItemStack constructor compatibility unverified
bugs.expected: If Holder<Item> does implement ItemLike on 1.21.2-1.21.10, ItemStack(it) compiles. If not, the fix is `.map { ItemStack(it.value()) }`. This is the same BuiltInRegistries.ITEM.get return-type concern raised by W2A5. Mark P3 informational — per-version smoke test (Task 7: 1.21.4) will confirm.
bugs.suggested_fix: At Task 7 smoke-test time, if :1.21.4:compileKotlin fails on this line, change to: .map { ItemStack(it.value()) }. No change needed now; this is a compile-time verification item, not a correctness bug in the authored code relative to the plan.
